Description: Hash passwords with Argon2id (OWASP recommendation) using spawn_blocking in async contexts
Do: Use argon2 crate with Argon2id variant; use SaltString::generate for random salts; store PHC-format strings; always spawn_blocking
Dont: Use bcrypt without tuning; hash on the async runtime thread; store raw hashes without salt

Description: Use jsonwebtoken crate for creating and verifying JSON Web Tokens with proper claims
Do: Set expiration times; use strong secrets (HS256) or public keys (RS256); validate all standard claims; rotate secrets
Dont: Store sensitive data in JWT payload; skip expiration validation; use weak secrets

Description: Use oauth2 crate for Authorization Code, Client Credentials, and PKCE flows
Do: Use PKCE for public clients; validate state parameter; exchange code for token on server side; store refresh tokens securely
Dont: Implement OAuth2 manually; skip CSRF protection (state parameter); store tokens in local storage

Description: Define roles and permissions as enums; check authorization in middleware or guard extractors
Do: Use typed roles; check permissions before executing business logic; log authorization failures; apply principle of least privilege
Dont: Use string-based role checks; skip authorization; only check authentication

Description: Use tower-sessions or axum-sessions for server-side session management with Redis backend
Do: Store session data server-side; use secure cookie settings; set proper expiration; use Redis for distributed sessions
Dont: Store session data in cookies; use predictable session IDs; skip secure flag
